mechanism_narrative: A persistently low unemployment rate (UNRATE) indicates a strong and stable labor market, which supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, thereby driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon. This is an always-on signal of economic health.
evidence_refs: fact:low unemployment generally correlates with strong equity markets
failure_condition: If UNRATE consistently shows an inverse relationship with SPX returns (i.e., higher UNRATE leads to higher returns) over multiple business cycles, or if the correlation is statistically insignificant (Rank IC < 0.03) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation, as measured by CPIAUCSL, rises significantly above a moderate level, it signals potential monetary tightening by the central bank. This tightening increases discount rates and reduces future earnings expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: If SPX forward returns remain positive or flat despite CPIAUCSL consistently exceeding the threshold for several quarters, or if central bank policy does not tighten in response to sustained high inflation.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of robust retail sales (RSAFS) growth and an accommodative federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) indicates strong consumer demand supported by loose monetary conditions. This environment is highly conducive to corporate profit growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: If periods of strong retail sales and low FEDFUNDS consistently coincide with negative or flat SPX returns, or if the interaction term between these two proxies shows no statistically significant predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of economic contraction, housing starts (HOUST) typically decline. A subsequent stabilization or slight uptick in HOUST, even at low levels, can signal an impending recovery in broader economic activity, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon as investor confidence returns.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month average change turns positive after a 6-month decline in GDPC1
failure_condition: If HOUST stabilization after a decline consistently fails to precede positive SPX returns during recovery periods, or if the signal is active but SPX returns are negative in > 60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects the health of the manufacturing sector and overall supply-side capacity utilization. This indicates a robust economy, which typically translates to higher corporate profits and positive S&P 500 returns over a weekly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:industrial output correlates with economic strength
failure_condition: If INDPRO growth consistently leads to negative SPX returns, or if the correlation between INDPRO growth and SPX returns is statistically insignificant (Rank IC < 0.03) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) from its recent low signals a deteriorating labor market and broader economic weakness. This typically precedes or coincides with economic downturns, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns due to reduced consumer spending and corporate profits over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by 0.5 percentage points or more from its 12-month low
failure_condition: If SPX forward returns remain positive or flat despite UNRATE consistently rising by the specified threshold, or if the signal triggers but no economic downturn follows within 6 months.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) is high and the unemployment rate (UNRATE) is also elevated, it suggests a challenging economic environment characterized by tight monetary policy despite weak labor markets. This combination typically leads to negative S&P 500 returns over a monthly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:high rates and high unemployment are recessionary
failure_condition: If SPX forward returns are consistently positive or flat when both FEDFUNDS and UNRATE exceed their respective thresholds, or if the interaction term shows no significant negative predictive power (t-statistic < 2.0).

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, excessively rapid GDP growth (GDPC1) can signal an overheating economy, which often precedes inflationary pressures and subsequent monetary tightening. This can lead to market corrections and negative S&P 500 returns over a weekly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:overheating economy can lead to corrections
failure_condition: If SPX forward returns remain consistently positive or flat during periods of very high GDPC1 growth, or if the signal triggers but no market correction follows within 3 months.
